Meta.title: Leandro Gómez Cano — Central American startup ecosystem
Meta.description: Building the startup ecosystem in Central America with a Nicaragua focus: business skills for founders, hands-on training, and build in public.
Meta.ogDescription: Startup ecosystem in Central America, Nicaragua-first. Founders with the skills to build real businesses—classes, roadmap, and build in public.
Meta.twitterDescription: Central America & Nicaragua: founders with the skills to win in business. Training, monthly challenge, and execution in the open.

RetoPage.title: Challenge
RetoPage.description: Monthly build-in-public challenge and product roadmap.

Hero.footnote: Hands-on classes, roadmap, and build in public—one way to plug into the ecosystem.

Challenge.title: Building in public, every month of the year

blocks.label: What it is
blocks.text: A commitment through December: each month a new product idea—from problem definition to launch and a minimum viable iteration.

blocks.label: Why
blocks.text: Speed forces decisions. Documenting in the open enforces clarity, cuts self-deception, and accelerates learning against real markets.

blocks.label: What we're building
blocks.text: Actionable prototypes and MVPs: tools, flows, and experiments that can be validated with users without empty narrative.

blocks.label: What we share
blocks.text: Scope decisions, trade-offs, metrics that matter, mistakes, and adjustments. No glamour: judgment and numbers when relevant.

blocks.label: What we'll learn
blocks.text: How to compress validation time, what to repeat in operations, and how to fold AI into the product cycle without losing business focus.

Classes.title: Hands-on training, going from idea to a tangible product.
Classes.subtitle: Sessions focused on tools and operational judgment. Workshops, 1:1 mentoring and special guests.

Classes.formMailSubject: First cohort priority — From Zero to App

items.title: From Zero to App
items.description: A fully hands-on program for founders with or without prior tech experience. Through a guided, step-by-step approach, you go from the basics of programming logic to building and publishing your own working application.
items.schedule: Starts May 19, 2026 · Tuesdays and Thursdays at 6:30 pm · online

syllabus.label: Week 1
syllabus.title: MVP definition, ICP, and smoke tests

syllabus.label: Week 4
syllabus.title: Introduction to GitHub, Vercel, and Supabase

syllabus.label: Week 6
syllabus.title: Build your first chatbot with Cursor

items.footnote: Enrollment: submit the priority form; we'll confirm payment options.

ClassesTeaser.title: A dedicated page for the full class offer.
ClassesTeaser.body: We split classes out of the home page so you can review the program, pricing, schedule, and priority form in one place.
ClassesTeaser.summary: From Zero to App is a hands-on cohort for founders who want to move from an idea to a published product with step-by-step guidance.
ClassesTeaser.schedule: Tuesdays and Thursdays at 6:30 pm · online

Community.title: Founders Club exists so you don't have to build alone.
Community.intro: The Founders Club community brings together founders and operators who want to move with more clarity, more speed, and less noise. It's a space to share progress, unblock decisions, and stay close to people who are building too.

pillars.title: Founder-to-founder connection
pillars.body: We want to bring together people building products, running businesses, and looking for peers who help them think better and execute faster.

pillars.title: Accountability and learning
pillars.body: The community is meant for sharing real progress, asking for feedback, and learning from the decisions, mistakes, and experiments others are already living through.

pillars.title: Momentum to execute
pillars.body: We don't want a passive community. The goal is to create a support point that turns conversations into action and projects into motion.

Community.ctaTitle: Join the Founders Club WhatsApp group
Community.ctaBody: If you want to stay close to the ecosystem, meet other founders, and join more direct conversations about product, business, and AI, this is the best place to start.
Community.ctaCardBody: Join the group to connect with the community, follow the ecosystem in real time, and participate in what we're building.

Roadmap.title: One launch per month · 2026
Roadmap.subtitle:
Remaining months of the year. Status and learnings update each cycle.
We will be building in public, going from idea to product in one month, every month of the year.

items.description: We're building a pet adoption matchmaking product: connect pets in need of a home with people and families through clear criteria, useful filters, and a lower-friction application flow.
items.keyLearning: Cycle in progress: validate matchmaking hypotheses and adoption criteria before scaling features.

detailSections.title: Block 0: Idea validation
detailSections.body: Initial assumptions about the problem, users, and product fit before going deeper into matchmaking design and the adoption flow.

detailSections.title: Block 1: Product direction
detailSections.body: The focus is matchmaking between pets in adoption and adopters: compatibility, transparent profiles, and an application process that protects both animals and people.

detailSections.title: Block 2: Decisions
detailSections.body: We prioritize adoption criteria and a full first match before adding secondary features.

ForWho.title: You don't need to know how to code

items.title: Founders & entrepreneurs
items.body: Who want to ship AI-powered products without relying on a full technical team from day one.

items.title: Operations teams
items.body: Looking to automate processes, improve customer service, and make decisions with real data.

items.title: Operators in LATAM
items.body: Working in markets where execution and speed matter.

items.q: Do I need to know how to code to take the classes?
items.a: No. Classes are designed for founders and teams without a technical background. We use no-code and low-code tools.

items.q: What's the difference between the classes, the monthly challenge, and the community?
items.a:
Classes are structured training in cohorts.

The challenge is a build-in-public exercise where we ship a new product every month and share what we learn—for free. We build the community to connect founders and push our projects forward.
Each class has a different price and duration. Contact us at +50588216400 for more information.
Fill out the priority form on the Classes page. We'll follow up with next steps and payment options.

items.q: Are classes online or in person?
items.a: Online, live via video call. Tuesdays and Thursdays at 6:30 PM.

Thesis.title: Ideas we're betting on
lines: Central America—and Nicaragua as a starting point—needs a stronger startup ecosystem; it's built one founder at a time.
AI changes how products are designed and shipped.
Latin America needs more operators who turn ideas into systems.
What you don't measure gets mistaken for progress.
Clarity is a competitive asset—you train it with explicit decisions.

FinalCta.title: Stop planning. Start building.
FinalCta.body: We grow the ecosystem through execution: follow the monthly challenge, join a class when it fits your stage, or watch how decisions happen in public.

Site.headline: We foster the startup ecosystem in Central America
Site.heroSub: We support founders with the skills and judgment to succeed in business: applied training, visible execution, and community.
